zh.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare於1480年出生在英國肯特托布利其。他於1505年與Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1550年在英國越可爾約克享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare于1480年出生在英国肯特托布利其。他于1505年与Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1550年在英国越可爾約克享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare was born in 1480, in Tonbridge, Kent, England. He married Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont in 1505, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1550, in York, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare est né en 1480, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre. Il a épousé Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont en 1505, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1550, à York, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare wurde 1480 in Tonbridge, Kent, England geboren. Er hat 1505 in England Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1550 im Alter von 70 Jahren in York, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare è nato il nel 1480 nel Tonbridge, Kent, England. Lui sposato Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont nel 1505, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1550, nel York, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clareは1480年にイギリスケントトンブリッジで生まれた。1505年にイギリスでElizabeth Isabel de Beaumontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1550年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヨークで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare(은)는 1480년 영국 켄트 토브리지에서 태어났다. 그는 1505년 영국에서 Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1550년 영국 요르크실르 요르크에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare nasceu em 1480, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont em 1505, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1550, em York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare родился в 1480 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont в 1505 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1550 г. в местности ерк, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Gilbert De Clare nació en 1480, en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra. Se casó con Elizabeth Isabel de Beaumont en 1505, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1550, en York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años.
